findings.description: charge_order logs the full PAN (card_number) and CVV in cleartext at INFO level, a direct PCI-DSS violation (CVV must never be retained post-auth; PAN must not be logged in cleartext) — CWE-532, OWASP A09.
charge_order accepts an arbitrary order_id with no ownership/authorization check, so any web caller can charge a card against any order — IDOR/missing authorization (CWE-639/CWE-862, OWASP A01).

refund(order_id) performs no authorization check and lets any caller mark any paid order 'refunded'; it also flips DB state without calling the gateway to move money, enabling fraud and reconciliation poisoning (CWE-862/CWE-840, OWASP A01/A06).

charge_order does not check the order's existing status or use an idempotency key before charging, so a replayed/retried request re-charges the card multiple times (double-charge / replay, OWASP A06).
monthly_revenue_report exposes aggregate financial revenue with no authorization/role check on the public entry point, leaking business-sensitive data to any caller (CWE-862, OWASP A01).

The gateway charge (external money movement) and the DB 'paid' update are non-atomic: if the UPDATE/commit fails after a successful charge, the customer is charged but the order is never marked paid, with no rollback/reconciliation (CWE-460, OWASP A10 partial failure).

Transaction ID is derived from the non-cryptographic Python hash() of only (last-4-of-card, amount_cents), making the persisted tx_id predictable and collision-prone for equal amount+last4 (CWE-330/CWE-340, OWASP A04).

PaymentGateway.charge takes CVV as a parameter, normalizing passing the CVV through the codebase; combined with the line-74 log this is the vector by which CVV reaches persistent storage/logs (CWE-316).

api_key is held as a plaintext instance attribute with no vault sourcing and no __repr__ redaction, so it can surface in logs/tracebacks given this file's verbose-logging habits (CWE-312/CWE-798-adjacent).
Monetary amounts use binary float arithmetic (unit_price: float, total*100) which accumulates rounding error; a correctness/precision issue rather than security — deferred to sibling critics.
